proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with feature_009 == 1 have more negative dependency_BRAF scores than cell lines with feature_009 == 0, after adjusting for the other local cell-line features.
Cell lines with feature_022 == 1 have more negative dependency_EGFR scores than cell lines with feature_022 == 0, after adjusting for the other local cell-line features.
Cell lines with feature_024 == 1 have more negative dependency_ERBB2 scores than cell lines with feature_024 == 0, after adjusting for the other local cell-line features.
Cell lines with feature_014 == 1 have more negative dependency_KRAS scores than cell lines with feature_014 == 0, after adjusting for the other local cell-line features.
Cell lines with feature_027 == 1 have more negative dependency_RIT1 scores than cell lines with feature_027 == 0, after adjusting for the other local cell-line features.

analyses.code: OLS(dependency_BRAF ~ all local features); coefficient for feature_009
analyses.result_summary: Additive OLS for dependency_BRAF gave coefficient -0.55428 for feature_009 after adjustment for all other local cell-line features (p=<1e-300). Negative values mean stronger dependency.

analyses.code: OLS(dependency_EGFR ~ all local features); coefficient for feature_022
analyses.result_summary: Additive OLS for dependency_EGFR gave coefficient -0.349177 for feature_022 after adjustment for all other local cell-line features (p=<1e-300). Negative values mean stronger dependency.

analyses.code: OLS(dependency_ERBB2 ~ all local features); coefficient for feature_024
analyses.result_summary: Additive OLS for dependency_ERBB2 gave coefficient -0.432718 for feature_024 after adjustment for all other local cell-line features (p=<1e-300). Negative values mean stronger dependency.

analyses.code: OLS(dependency_KRAS ~ all local features); coefficient for feature_014
analyses.result_summary: Additive OLS for dependency_KRAS gave coefficient -0.309702 for feature_014 after adjustment for all other local cell-line features (p=<1e-300). Negative values mean stronger dependency.

analyses.code: OLS(dependency_RIT1 ~ all local features); coefficient for feature_027
analyses.result_summary: Additive OLS for dependency_RIT1 gave coefficient -0.0954692 for feature_027 after adjustment for all other local cell-line features (p=6.30e-69). Negative values mean stronger dependency.

iterations.update: Strong binary-feature main effects were present for BRAF, EGFR, ERBB2, KRAS, and RIT1, motivating targeted modifier checks.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with feature_011 == colorectal have more negative dependency_KIF18A scores than non-colorectal lines, after adjusting for the other local cell-line features.
Higher feature_016 is associated with more negative dependency_POLR2A scores, with a negative effect per one-standard-deviation increase after adjustment for other local features.
Higher feature_003 is associated with more negative dependency_RPL11 scores, with a negative effect per one-standard-deviation increase after adjustment for other local features.
Higher feature_023 is associated with more negative dependency_DHX9 scores, with a negative effect per one-standard-deviation increase after adjustment for other local features.
Higher feature_016 is associated with more negative dependency_TMED10 scores, with a negative effect per one-standard-deviation increase after adjustment for other local features.

analyses.code: OLS(dependency_KIF18A ~ all local features); coefficient for feature_011_colorectal
analyses.result_summary: Additive OLS for dependency_KIF18A gave coefficient -0.371385 for feature_011_colorectal after adjustment for all other local cell-line features (p=<1e-300). Negative values mean stronger dependency.

analyses.code: OLS(dependency_POLR2A ~ all local features); coefficient for feature_016
analyses.result_summary: Additive OLS for dependency_POLR2A gave coefficient -0.0613987 for feature_016 after adjustment for all other local cell-line features (p=<1e-300). Negative values mean stronger dependency.

analyses.code: OLS(dependency_RPL11 ~ all local features); coefficient for feature_003
analyses.result_summary: Additive OLS for dependency_RPL11 gave coefficient -0.0558207 for feature_003 after adjustment for all other local cell-line features (p=1.34e-120). Negative values mean stronger dependency.

analyses.code: OLS(dependency_DHX9 ~ all local features); coefficient for feature_023
analyses.result_summary: Additive OLS for dependency_DHX9 gave coefficient -0.0169881 for feature_023 after adjustment for all other local cell-line features (p=1.84e-24). Negative values mean stronger dependency.

analyses.code: OLS(dependency_TMED10 ~ all local features); coefficient for feature_016
analyses.result_summary: Additive OLS for dependency_TMED10 gave coefficient -0.0203336 for feature_016 after adjustment for all other local cell-line features (p=2.23e-38). Negative values mean stronger dependency.

iterations.update: KIF18A showed a large colorectal association, while POLR2A, RPL11, DHX9, and TMED10 showed smaller continuous-feature associations.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative dependency_BRAF effect of feature_009 == 1 is stronger in feature_011 == skin cell lines than in non-skin cell lines, so the feature_009-by-skin interaction is negative.
The negative dependency_EGFR effect of feature_022 == 1 is stronger when feature_023 >= 5.669 than when feature_023 is lower, so the feature_022-by-high-feature_023 interaction is negative.

The negative dependency_ERBB2 effect of feature_024 == 1 is stronger in feature_010 == her2_enriched cell lines than in other feature_010 subgroups, so the feature_024-by-her2_enriched interaction is negative.
The negative dependency_KRAS effect of feature_014 == 1 is stronger in feature_011 == pancreatic cell lines than in non-pancreatic lines, so the feature_014-by-pancreatic interaction is negative.
The negative dependency_RIT1 effect of feature_027 == 1 is stronger in feature_011 == brain cell lines than in non-brain lines, so the feature_027-by-brain interaction is negative.

analyses.code: OLS(dependency_BRAF ~ driver + modifier + driver:modifier)
analyses.result_summary: OLS interaction for feature_009 == 1 by feature_011 == skin on dependency_BRAF: interaction coefficient -0.0126368; driver effect was -0.564033 within the modifier and -0.551396 outside it (p=0.2883).

analyses.code: OLS(dependency_EGFR ~ driver + modifier + driver:modifier)
analyses.result_summary: OLS interaction for feature_022 == 1 by feature_023 >= 5.669 on dependency_EGFR: interaction coefficient -0.0299141; driver effect was -0.371308 within the modifier and -0.341394 outside it (p=0.0280).

analyses.code: OLS(dependency_ERBB2 ~ driver + modifier + driver:modifier)
analyses.result_summary: OLS interaction for feature_024 == 1 by feature_010 == her2_enriched on dependency_ERBB2: interaction coefficient -0.0671075; driver effect was -0.497263 within the modifier and -0.430156 outside it (p=0.0372).

analyses.code: OLS(dependency_KRAS ~ driver + modifier + driver:modifier)
analyses.result_summary: OLS interaction for feature_014 == 1 by feature_011 == pancreatic on dependency_KRAS: interaction coefficient -0.00279815; driver effect was -0.310173 within the modifier and -0.307374 outside it (p=0.7948).

analyses.code: OLS(dependency_RIT1 ~ driver + modifier + driver:modifier)
analyses.result_summary: OLS interaction for feature_027 == 1 by feature_011 == brain on dependency_RIT1: interaction coefficient -0.0582286; driver effect was -0.14955 within the modifier and -0.0913211 outside it (p=0.0056).

iterations.update: Most oncogene-driver effects were broad across lineages. BRAF was not skin-specific and KRAS was not pancreatic-specific; ERBB2 and RIT1 had modest subgroup enhancement.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with feature_011 == colorectal, feature_035 == 1, and feature_020 == 0 have markedly more negative dependency_KIF18A scores than all other cell lines.
Within feature_011 == colorectal and feature_035 == 1 cell lines, feature_020 == 1 suppresses KIF18A dependency, giving less negative dependency_KIF18A scores than feature_020 == 0.
Within the colorectal feature_035 KIF18A subgroup, feature_028 >= 1.427 further increases dependency_KIF18A, giving a negative colorectal-by-feature_035-by-high-feature_028 interaction.
Cell lines satisfying feature_011 == colorectal, feature_035 == 1, feature_020 == 0, and feature_028 >= 1.427 have the most negative dependency_KIF18A scores relative to all other cell lines.

analyses.code: Welch t-test comparing feature_011 == colorectal AND feature_035 == 1 AND feature_020 == 0 against the complement for dependency_KIF18A
analyses.result_summary: feature_011 == colorectal AND feature_035 == 1 AND feature_020 == 0: mean dependency_KIF18A was -1.09223 in 3226 matching lines vs -0.25266 in the complement; difference -0.839575 (Welch p=<1e-300).

analyses.code: Welch t-test within feature_011 == colorectal and feature_035 == 1: feature_020 == 1 vs feature_020 == 0
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_011 == colorectal and feature_035 == 1, feature_020 == 1 had mean dependency_KIF18A -0.251623 in 699 lines vs -1.09223 for feature_020 == 0 in 3226 lines; positive difference 0.840612 indicates suppression of the negative dependency (Welch p=<1e-300).

analyses.code: OLS(dependency_KIF18A ~ colorectal + feature_035 + feature_020 + high_feature_028 + lower-order terms + colorectal:feature_035:high_feature_028)
analyses.result_summary: Modeling dependency_KIF18A with colorectal, feature_035, feature_020, feature_028 >= 1.427, and lower-order interactions gave colorectal:feature_035:high-feature_028 coefficient -0.512134 (p=1.85e-67).

analyses.code: Welch t-test comparing feature_011 == colorectal AND feature_035 == 1 AND feature_020 == 0 AND feature_028 >= 1.427 against the complement for dependency_KIF18A
analyses.result_summary: feature_011 == colorectal AND feature_035 == 1 AND feature_020 == 0 AND feature_028 >= 1.427: mean dependency_KIF18A was -1.3952 in 1732 matching lines vs -0.267776 in the complement; difference -1.12742 (Welch p=<1e-300).

iterations.update: KIF18A had the clearest multivariable dependency subgroup: colorectal plus feature_035 == 1, enabled by feature_020 == 0 and intensified by high feature_028.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a systematic one- and two-predicate heterogeneity screen, cell lines matching the top rule feature_009 == 1 have more negative dependency_BRAF scores than the complement.

In a systematic one- and two-predicate heterogeneity screen, cell lines matching the top rule feature_022 == 1 AND feature_016 >= q90 (1.09) have more negative dependency_EGFR scores than the complement.
In a systematic one- and two-predicate heterogeneity screen, cell lines matching the top rule feature_024 == 1 have more negative dependency_ERBB2 scores than the complement.
In a systematic one- and two-predicate heterogeneity screen, cell lines matching the top rule feature_014 == 1 have more negative dependency_KRAS scores than the complement.
In a systematic one- and two-predicate heterogeneity screen, cell lines matching the top rule feature_011 == brain AND feature_027 == 1 have more negative dependency_RIT1 scores than the complement.

In a systematic one- and two-predicate heterogeneity screen, cell lines matching the top rule feature_011 == colorectal AND feature_028 >= q90 (1.43) have more negative dependency_KIF18A scores than the complement.

In a systematic one- and two-predicate heterogeneity screen, cell lines matching the top rule feature_026 >= q90 (1.15) AND feature_016 >= q90 (1.09) have more negative dependency_TMED10 scores than the complement.

In a systematic one- and two-predicate heterogeneity screen, cell lines matching the top rule feature_011 == ovarian AND feature_016 >= q90 (1.09) have more negative dependency_DHX9 scores than the complement.

In a systematic one- and two-predicate heterogeneity screen, cell lines matching the top rule feature_016 >= q90 (1.09) AND feature_015 <= q10 (2.08) have more negative dependency_POLR2A scores than the complement.

In a systematic one- and two-predicate heterogeneity screen, cell lines matching the top rule feature_022 == 1 AND feature_003 >= q90 (1) have more negative dependency_RPL11 scores than the complement.

analyses.code: Enumerate categorical levels, binary values, and continuous quantile predicates; test eligible one- and two-predicate subgroups with Welch tests; BH-adjust within outcome.
analyses.result_summary: Systematic screen over 120 primitive predicates and 6671 eligible one-/two-predicate rules for dependency_BRAF selected feature_009 == 1: mean -0.804698 in 5574 lines vs -0.249503 in the complement; effect -0.555195, raw p=<1e-300, BH q=<1e-300.

analyses.code: Enumerate categorical levels, binary values, and continuous quantile predicates; test eligible one- and two-predicate subgroups with Welch tests; BH-adjust within outcome.
analyses.result_summary: Systematic screen over 120 primitive predicates and 6671 eligible one-/two-predicate rules for dependency_EGFR selected feature_022 == 1 AND feature_016 >= q90 (1.09): mean -0.64743 in 393 lines vs -0.276547 in the complement; effect -0.370883, raw p=1.66e-65, BH q=1.28e-63.

analyses.code: Enumerate categorical levels, binary values, and continuous quantile predicates; test eligible one- and two-predicate subgroups with Welch tests; BH-adjust within outcome.
analyses.result_summary: Systematic screen over 120 primitive predicates and 6671 eligible one-/two-predicate rules for dependency_ERBB2 selected feature_024 == 1: mean -0.685595 in 4890 lines vs -0.253473 in the complement; effect -0.432122, raw p=<1e-300, BH q=<1e-300.

analyses.code: Enumerate categorical levels, binary values, and continuous quantile predicates; test eligible one- and two-predicate subgroups with Welch tests; BH-adjust within outcome.
analyses.result_summary: Systematic screen over 120 primitive predicates and 6671 eligible one-/two-predicate rules for dependency_KRAS selected feature_014 == 1: mean -0.559415 in 11842 lines vs -0.251959 in the complement; effect -0.307456, raw p=<1e-300, BH q=<1e-300.

analyses.code: Enumerate categorical levels, binary values, and continuous quantile predicates; test eligible one- and two-predicate subgroups with Welch tests; BH-adjust within outcome.
analyses.result_summary: Systematic screen over 120 primitive predicates and 6671 eligible one-/two-predicate rules for dependency_RIT1 selected feature_011 == brain AND feature_027 == 1: mean -0.404687 in 329 lines vs -0.264988 in the complement; effect -0.139699, raw p=1.01e-12, BH q=7.03e-11.

analyses.code: Enumerate categorical levels, binary values, and continuous quantile predicates; test eligible one- and two-predicate subgroups with Welch tests; BH-adjust within outcome.
analyses.result_summary: Systematic screen over 120 primitive predicates and 6671 eligible one-/two-predicate rules for dependency_KIF18A selected feature_011 == colorectal AND feature_028 >= q90 (1.43): mean -1.06501 in 2454 lines vs -0.267698 in the complement; effect -0.797309, raw p=<1e-300, BH q=<1e-300.

analyses.code: Enumerate categorical levels, binary values, and continuous quantile predicates; test eligible one- and two-predicate subgroups with Welch tests; BH-adjust within outcome.
analyses.result_summary: Systematic screen over 120 primitive predicates and 6671 eligible one-/two-predicate rules for dependency_TMED10 selected feature_026 >= q90 (1.15) AND feature_016 >= q90 (1.09): mean -0.342437 in 490 lines vs -0.253129 in the complement; effect -0.0893073, raw p=2.16e-08, BH q=1.62e-06.

analyses.code: Enumerate categorical levels, binary values, and continuous quantile predicates; test eligible one- and two-predicate subgroups with Welch tests; BH-adjust within outcome.
analyses.result_summary: Systematic screen over 120 primitive predicates and 6671 eligible one-/two-predicate rules for dependency_DHX9 selected feature_011 == ovarian AND feature_016 >= q90 (1.09): mean -0.418326 in 435 lines vs -0.332924 in the complement; effect -0.0854023, raw p=9.36e-07, BH q=2.92e-05.

analyses.code: Enumerate categorical levels, binary values, and continuous quantile predicates; test eligible one- and two-predicate subgroups with Welch tests; BH-adjust within outcome.
analyses.result_summary: Systematic screen over 120 primitive predicates and 6671 eligible one-/two-predicate rules for dependency_POLR2A selected feature_016 >= q90 (1.09) AND feature_015 <= q10 (2.08): mean -0.556536 in 513 lines vs -0.41765 in the complement; effect -0.138886, raw p=1.03e-17, BH q=2.54e-16.

analyses.code: Enumerate categorical levels, binary values, and continuous quantile predicates; test eligible one- and two-predicate subgroups with Welch tests; BH-adjust within outcome.
analyses.result_summary: Systematic screen over 120 primitive predicates and 6671 eligible one-/two-predicate rules for dependency_RPL11 selected feature_022 == 1 AND feature_003 >= q90 (1): mean -0.424703 in 354 lines vs -0.268801 in the complement; effect -0.155903, raw p=1.12e-14, BH q=1.86e-13.

iterations.update: A systematic one- and two-predicate heterogeneity screen was run for every dependency outcome. It confirmed strong driver-dominated rules for oncogene outcomes and highlighted weaker pairwise enrichments for TMED10, DHX9, POLR2A, and RPL11.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with feature_016 >= 1.088 and feature_026 >= 1.153 have more negative dependency_TMED10 scores than all other cell lines.
Cell lines with feature_016 >= 1.088 and feature_023 >= 6.330 have more negative dependency_DHX9 scores than all other cell lines.
Cell lines with feature_016 >= 1.088 and feature_015 <= 2.083 have more negative dependency_POLR2A scores than all other cell lines.
Cell lines with feature_003 >= 1.003 and feature_018 == 1 have more negative dependency_RPL11 scores than all other cell lines.

analyses.code: Welch t-test comparing feature_016 >= 1.088 AND feature_026 >= 1.153 against the complement for dependency_TMED10
analyses.result_summary: feature_016 >= 1.088 AND feature_026 >= 1.153: mean dependency_TMED10 was -0.342437 in 490 matching lines vs -0.253129 in the complement; difference -0.0893073 (Welch p=2.16e-08).

analyses.code: Welch t-test comparing feature_016 >= 1.088 AND feature_023 >= 6.330 against the complement for dependency_DHX9
analyses.result_summary: feature_016 >= 1.088 AND feature_023 >= 6.330: mean dependency_DHX9 was -0.417384 in 571 matching lines vs -0.3327 in the complement; difference -0.0846835 (Welch p=7.51e-08).

analyses.code: Welch t-test comparing feature_016 >= 1.088 AND feature_015 <= 2.083 against the complement for dependency_POLR2A
analyses.result_summary: feature_016 >= 1.088 AND feature_015 <= 2.083: mean dependency_POLR2A was -0.556536 in 513 matching lines vs -0.41765 in the complement; difference -0.138886 (Welch p=1.03e-17).

analyses.code: Welch t-test comparing feature_003 >= 1.003 AND feature_018 == 1 against the complement for dependency_RPL11
analyses.result_summary: feature_003 >= 1.003 AND feature_018 == 1: mean dependency_RPL11 was -0.384459 in 4919 matching lines vs -0.257405 in the complement; difference -0.127054 (Welch p=4.19e-120).

iterations.update: Focused subgroup tests for the weaker/common dependencies showed statistically significant subgroup mean shifts, though effect sizes were much smaller than the oncogene and KIF18A patterns.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete KIF18A rule feature_011 == colorectal AND feature_035 == 1 AND feature_020 == 0 AND feature_028 >= 1.427 remains associated with more negative dependency_KIF18A after additive adjustment for all local features.
The TMED10 rule feature_016 >= 1.088 AND feature_026 >= 1.153 remains associated with more negative dependency_TMED10 after additive adjustment for all local features.
The POLR2A rule feature_016 >= 1.088 AND feature_015 <= 2.083 remains associated with more negative dependency_POLR2A after additive adjustment for all local features.
The DHX9 rule feature_016 >= 1.088 AND feature_023 >= 6.330 remains associated with more negative dependency_DHX9 after additive adjustment for all local features.
The RPL11 rule feature_003 >= 1.003 AND feature_018 == 1 remains associated with more negative dependency_RPL11 after additive adjustment for all local features.

analyses.code: OLS(dependency_KIF18A ~ all local features + rule_kif18a_colorectal_f035_f0200_f028hi90)
analyses.result_summary: After adding the rule indicator rule_kif18a_colorectal_f035_f0200_f028hi90 to the all-feature additive OLS model for dependency_KIF18A, residual rule coefficient was -0.921441 (p=<1e-300).

analyses.code: OLS(dependency_TMED10 ~ all local features + rule_tmed10_f016hi90_f026hi90)
analyses.result_summary: After adding the rule indicator rule_tmed10_f016hi90_f026hi90 to the all-feature additive OLS model for dependency_TMED10, residual rule coefficient was -0.0334248 (p=0.0413).

analyses.code: OLS(dependency_POLR2A ~ all local features + rule_polr2a_f016hi90_f015lo10)
analyses.result_summary: After adding the rule indicator rule_polr2a_f016hi90_f015lo10 to the all-feature additive OLS model for dependency_POLR2A, residual rule coefficient was -0.0315137 (p=0.0471).

analyses.code: OLS(dependency_DHX9 ~ all local features + rule_dhx9_f016hi90_f023hi90)
analyses.result_summary: After adding the rule indicator rule_dhx9_f016hi90_f023hi90 to the all-feature additive OLS model for dependency_DHX9, residual rule coefficient was -0.0185179 (p=0.2249).

analyses.code: OLS(dependency_RPL11 ~ all local features + rule_rpl11_f003hi90_f018)
analyses.result_summary: After adding the rule indicator rule_rpl11_f003hi90_f018 to the all-feature additive OLS model for dependency_RPL11, residual rule coefficient was 0.00124956 (p=0.8825).

iterations.update: Residual rule models showed the complete KIF18A interaction remained very strong beyond additive features; TMED10 and POLR2A residual rules were modest, while DHX9 and RPL11 pair rules were mostly additive rather than independent interactions.
